title: LED Driver (Boost Stage)
description: A boost converter that raises a USB 5 V supply to 12 V for driving a series string of white LEDs at approximately 350 mA. Demonstrates the boost topology for above-rail LED applications.
design_notes: This design covers only the voltage conversion stage. True constant-current LED control requires a dedicated CC feedback loop (a sense resistor in the LED return path feeding back to the controller's FB pin, or a dedicated LED driver IC). The 300 kHz switching frequency shrinks the inductor to a practical 47–68 µH footprint. The 2 % output ripple (240 mV) is acceptable for LEDs because the LED's forward-voltage characteristic acts as a soft limiter — tighter regulation is only needed when PWM dimming with a precise average-current target. Boost converters require a zero-ESR output capacitor; use an X5R or X7R ceramic, not electrolytic.
source: TI SLVA372D — Understanding Boost Power Stages in Switchmode Power Supplies
